name: Oyster-Stuffed Pork Loin with Apple Cider Cre
recipeInstructions:
1. Spiral cut pork loin until it is shaped rectangular and about 1/2 inch thick.
2. In a saucepan, brown onions, celery, and bell pepper.
3. Add oysters and oyster liquid. Cook for 3-4 minutes.
4. Add seafood base and heavy cream. Cook for 5 minutes.
5. Stir in breadcrumbs to thicken the mixture. If too thick, add more cream and adjust seasonings.
6. Cool the mixture.
7. Spread it evenly over the pork loin.
8. Starting at one end, roll the pork loin tightly to give a jellyroll effect.
9. Tie with a butcher's knot.
10. Roll in crushed red peppercorns.
11. Bake at 350°F until brown and tender. Cut into medallions.
12. Place apple cider cream sauce on a serving plate.
13. Arrange the medallions on top of the sauce.
